creator: anders@fix.no
text:
And, instead, this'll take the port to patchlevel 08, which is the latest
as of today:

diff -Nur postfix.old/Makefile postfix/Makefile
--- postfix.old/Makefile	Fri Sep 10 23:48:58 1999
+++ postfix/Makefile	Thu Nov 25 19:38:25 1999
@@ -6,8 +6,8 @@
 # $FreeBSD: ports/mail/postfix/Makefile,v 1.9 1999/09/10 21:48:58 torstenb Exp $
 #
 
-DISTNAME=	postfix-19990906-pl02
-PKGNAME=	postfix-19990906.02
+DISTNAME=	postfix-19990906-pl08
+PKGNAME=	postfix-19990906.08
 CATEGORIES=	mail
 MASTER_SITES=	ftp://ftp.merit.edu/postfix/official/ \
 		ftp://ftp.snoopy.net/pub/mirrors/postfix/official/ \
diff -Nur postfix.old/files/md5 postfix/files/md5
--- postfix.old/files/md5	Fri Sep 10 22:41:44 1999
+++ postfix/files/md5	Thu Nov 25 19:40:42 1999
@@ -1 +1 @@
-MD5 (postfix-19990906-pl02.tar.gz) = 7d712bc007bcb05728347cb6a3fbd2be
+MD5 (postfix-19990906-pl08.tar.gz) = 820eba2bdbe635853e4984d823b56258

I'd be happy to take over the maintenance of this port, if Torsten wants
me to. Postfix sure does deserve faster updates in our ports collection.

I'm using Postfix on several servers now, and it seems to be the most
secure, efficient, consistent and _compatible_ alternative today.

-- 
Anders Nordby ^ anders@fix.no ^ http://anders.fix.no/
